seoTitle: How to Change Microsoft Teams Display Name Easily (2026)
metaDescription: Learn how to update your Microsoft Teams display name for personal accounts, work accounts, or temporarily during a live meeting.
intro: How you update your Teams display name depends heavily on whether you're using a personal Microsoft account or a managed work account. This guide covers editing your name through the Microsoft account settings for personal profiles, understanding directory restrictions for work accounts, and using the in-meeting rename feature for a temporary one-time change. It's aimed at anyone who's hit a locked name field and isn't sure why.

steps.heading: Open your profile settings
steps.body: Click your profile avatar in the top right of Teams to access account settings.

steps.heading: Identify your account type
steps.body: Check whether you're using a personal Microsoft account or a managed work or school account, since editing options differ.

steps.heading: Edit a personal account name
steps.body: Select the profile card link to redirect to the browser-based Microsoft account info panel, click edit name, and save your changes.

steps.heading: Restart Teams
steps.body: Force-restart the Teams app to trigger a server sync so your updated profile name appears everywhere.

steps.heading: Rename temporarily in a meeting
steps.body: Join a meeting without logging in, or open the in-meeting People panel and select edit display name to set a one-time call identity.

faq.question: Why can't I change my display name in Teams?
faq.answer: If you're on a managed work or school account, your name is often controlled by your organization's directory and may require an IT administrator to update.

faq.question: Do I need to restart Teams after changing my name?
faq.answer: Yes, force-restarting the app helps trigger a server sync so the updated profile name reflects across the platform.

faq.question: Can I use a different name just for one meeting?
faq.answer: Yes, joining without logging in or using the in-meeting People panel's edit display name option lets you set a temporary name for that session only.

faq.question: Does changing my personal account name affect other Microsoft apps?
faq.answer: Yes, since the change is made at the Microsoft account level, it can flow through to other connected Microsoft services as well.
